Contact Rare & Manuscript Collections

Cornell University faculty biographical files, 1865-2004.
Collection Number: 47-10-3394

Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections
Cornell University Library

DESCRIPTIVE SUMMARY

Title:
Cornell University faculty biographical files, 1865-2004.
Repository:
Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections
Collection Number:
47-10-3394
Abstract:
Correspondence, photographs, clippings, and biographical data concerning Cornell faculty and some staff.
Creator:
Cornell University. Library. Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections
Quanitities:
66 cubic feet.
Language:
Collection material in English
COLLECTION DESCRIPTION

Correspondence, photographs, clippings, and biographical data concerning Cornell faculty and some staff.
INFORMATION FOR USERS
Cite As:

Cornell University Faculty Biographical files, #47-10-3394. Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections, Cornell University Library.
Related Materials

For alumni who were not faculty, but were Cornell trustees, benefactors, or other notables, such as George Boldt, Mary Merritt Crawford, Herbert Fisk Johnson, Samuel Curtis Johnson, Carl A. Kroch, Helen Newman, Floyd Newman, John Merrill Olin, Spencer T. Olin, Robert W. Purcell, Myron Charles Taylor, Willard Straight, Walter Clark Teagle, Charles E. Treman Jr., Robert Henry Treman, see their file in the Deceased Alumni Collection, #41-2-877. Cornell faculty members who were also alumni may also have an additional folder in the Deceased Alumni Collection as well.
Related Materials

See also the Faculty Memorial Statements, https://ecommons.cornell.edu/handle/1813/17811 and Faculty Legacy collection https://ecommons.cornell.edu/handle/1813/14143.
SUBJECTS

Names:
Cornell University -- : Faculty.
Cornell University -- : Employees.
Cornell University -- : Administration.
Cornell University -- : Alumni and alumnae.
Form and Genre Terms:
Photographs.

Scope and Contents
Includes a copy of The Fox Chronicles, stories of Saint Francis, the Great White Healer, that documents many of the stories and pranks that Fox was known for.
